name: Joe Franklin
abstract: Joe Franklin (March 9, 1926 – January 24, 2015), born Joseph Fortgang, was an American radio and television host personality, author and actor from New York City. Franklin is noted for having the first talk show and inventing the format.

concerts.label: Fashion Show presented by Raven Chanticleer
A Night in a Neapolitan Cafe, featuring Carmine Gagliardi

about_quotes.speaker: William Randolph "Cozy" Cole
about_quotes.evidence: Joe Franklin is mentioned by the interviewer as a TV host; the interviewee does not respond or comment on any personal interaction.

about_quotes.speaker: Vince Giordano
about_quotes.evidence: And even watching T.V. shows like Joe Franklin, who was a fellow here in New York who is still around, and Max Morath.
about_quotes.doc_title: Vince Giordano interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 11, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Chuck Green
about_quotes.evidence: I went on the television with Joe Franklin, you see. So, it happened that once, and then I got invited to return.

about_quotes.speaker: Carmen Leggio
about_quotes.evidence: Joe Franklin I did on my own about two or three times, he interviewed me at least two or three times
about_quotes.doc_title: Carmen Leggio interviewed by Monk Rowe, Tarrytown, New York, October 31, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Tiny Grimes
about_quotes.evidence: And the bass player, I think his name was Joe Franklin. He said, 'Play man, play.'
